meta.disclaimer: Reasoned analytical estimates from live team-news research, not live bookmaker prices. Fun-money only — always check the actual odds before you stake.
meta.method: Per-match deep research on both squads (probable XIs, form, injuries, set-piece + penalty takers) via live web search, refreshed daily and again near kickoff when lineups are confirmed.

win.reason: Opta supercomputer gives Portugal 54.5% win vs 23.2% DR Congo, 22.3% draw. Portugal unbeaten in last 5 (3 straight friendly wins pre-tournament), arguably best midfield in the tournament (Vitinha, B.Fernandes, J.Neves, Bernardo Silva) plus deep attack. DR Congo set up to defend deep in a compact 4-5-1/5-3-2 and counter, which caps Portugal's margin but not the result.

scorers.note: Captain at his sixth WC, top scorer in Portugal's qualifying group (5 goals), penalty taker, scored in WC openers before (Ghana 2022). Central striker in Martinez's 4-2-3-1.
Sharp PSG finisher and the natural No.9 alternative/sub; reliable late-game scorer if Ronaldo is rotated or Portugal chase a second-half goal vs a tiring deep block.
Back in his favoured No.10 role, on penalties/free-kicks as backup, arrives in the box; record 21 PL assists this season but also a goal threat from range.
Direct wide threat cutting in on his stronger foot against a back five; one-on-one dribbler likely to find space late as DR Congo's block fatigues.

assists.note: Primary creator from the No.10 role; league's standout assist man (21 PL assists), main set-piece and through-ball supplier to Ronaldo.
Overlapping left-back delivering cutbacks/crosses against a compact block; one of Portugal's most advanced full-back outlets.
Deep-lying playmaker controlling tempo; line-breaking passes to release Neto/Bernardo and feed the box against a low block.

penalty.note: DR Congo defend deep with a packed box (Wan-Bissaka, Mbemba, Tuanzebe, Masuaku), raising the chance of a box foul over 90 minutes. Ronaldo is the established first-choice penalty taker, Bruno Fernandes the reliable backup.

lineups.home: Diogo Costa, Joao Cancelo, Tomas Araujo, Ruben Dias, Nuno Mendes, Joao Neves, Vitinha, Bernardo Silva, Bruno Fernandes, Joao Felix, Cristiano Ronaldo
lineups.away: Lionel Mpasi, Aaron Wan-Bissaka, Chancel Mbemba, Axel Tuanzebe, Arthur Masuaku, Meschack Elia, Samuel Moutoussamy, Noah Sadiki, Nathanael Mbuku, Cedric Bakambu, Yoane Wissa

playerNotes.note: 41, sixth World Cup, 226 caps / 143 goals (both records). First-choice penalty taker; led qualifying scoring with 5. The match's headline figure and main goal threat.
Back in his preferred No.10; record-breaking PL assist season. Primary creator, on free-kicks and backup penalties.
Defensive leader; had injury problems in the second half of the club season but is fit for the opener and expected to start at centre-back.
Star man and main attacking outlet; 9 international goals. Injury-hit, underwhelming Newcastle season, but DR Congo's chief counter-attacking threat.
Veteran striker, squad's top scorer (21 international goals); big-game experience, likely to feature as a focal point or impact option off the bench.
Premier League full-back anchoring a likely back five; key to containing Portugal's wide overloads from Neto and Nuno Mendes.

win.reason: England field their strongest XI with Kane in career-best form (61 goals for Bayern in 2025-26) and a deeper squad. Croatia leaving Kovacic on the bench weakens their midfield control against the Rice-Anderson pivot. England favored but Croatia's tournament experience and Modric keep it from being a rout.

scorers.note: England's penalty taker and the most prolific striker in the tournament (61 goals club 2025-26); central reference point and primary goal threat.
Driving from the No.10 role, arrives late in the box behind Kane; secondary goal threat from deep runs.
Starts on the left wing; pace in behind Croatia's back three, cutting inside onto his stronger foot.
Croatia's lone starting striker in red-hot form (12 in 13 MLS); plays his club football at this very AT&T Stadium for FC Dallas — main away threat fed by Baturina.

assists.note: Primary creator from the No.10, feeding Kane and the wingers.
Right-back and a corner/free-kick deliverer for England; overlaps Madueke and whips crosses for Kane.
Croatia's chief creative outlet in the advanced role; the designated supplier to Musa and a set-piece option.

penalty.backup: Bukayo Saka (England, from bench) / Ivan Perisic (Croatia)
penalty.note: Kane is England's automatic spot-kick taker. With Kovacic and Kramaric benched, Modric is Croatia's on-pitch penalty taker, with Perisic the next option and Budimir the bench backup. Modric, Perisic, Pasalic and Baturina share set-piece duties.

lineups.home: Jordan Pickford, Reece James, John Stones, Ezri Konsa, Nico O'Reilly, Elliot Anderson, Declan Rice, Noni Madueke, Jude Bellingham, Anthony Gordon, Harry Kane
lineups.away: Dominik Livakovic, Josip Sutalo, Luka Vuskovic, Josko Gvardiol, Josip Stanisic, Luka Sucic, Luka Modric, Ivan Perisic, Mario Pasalic, Martin Baturina, Petar Musa

playerNotes.note: Career-best form, 61 goals for Bayern in 2025-26 and second European Golden Shoe; penalty taker and focal point.
Starts at centre-back alongside Stones; Marc Guehi drops to the bench — a notable selection call.
Starts at left-back, the least-experienced England starter; Croatia may target his flank with Stanisic and Pasalic.
Captains at 40 after recovering from a cheekbone fracture; controls midfield and is the on-pitch penalty taker with Kovacic benched.
Preferred over Kramaric at striker; plays his MLS football at AT&T Stadium for FC Dallas, a familiarity edge in the venue.
BENCHED despite featuring in predicted XIs; his absence from the engine room weakens Croatia's ball progression against Rice and Anderson.

win.reason: Ghana carry the clear individual-quality edge in attack with in-form Semenyo (Premier League standout, market-leading ~30% anytime scorer probability) plus captain Jordan Ayew (7 goals in qualifying) and Nuamah running wide. Panama, despite Partey (visa) and Kudus (injury) being out, still face a Ghana front line that is more dangerous than the surprise Waterman-Barcenas pairing Christiansen has picked. Panama benching Fajardo, Diaz, Davis and Godoy weakens their central goal threat and set-piece pedigree, leaving them reliant on counters; Ghana should edge a tight, lower-scoring opener.

scorers.note: Unanimous market leader (~+230, 29.7% implied); virtually all Ghana's final-third output runs through him with Partey and Kudus absent.
Captain, penalty taker and qualifying talisman with 7 goals in 10 - Ghana's central focal point up top.
Panama's surprise starter and market-leading scorer threat (~+220/+300), 14 international goals, the counter-attack outlet up top.
Wide forward at ~4/1, direct runner who stretches Panama's deep block from the flank.

assists.note: Creates as much as he scores; Fox Sports flagged a Semenyo goal+assist double as a live angle.
Added 7 assists alongside 7 goals in qualifying - Ghana's primary creator-finisher link.
Panama's main transition outlet and a free-kick/corner taker now Davis and Carrasquilla are benched.

penalty.note: With listed pen-takers Davis and Carrasquilla both benched, Waterman is the on-pitch penalty option for Panama; for Ghana, Jordan Ayew remains the designated taker with a flawless senior record.

lineups.home: Ati-Zigi, Senaya, Adjetey, Opoku, Mensah, Owusu, Yirenkyi, Nuamah, Boakye, Semenyo, Ayew
lineups.away: Mosquera, Blackman, Cordoba, Ramos, Andrade, Murillo, Martinez, Harvey, Rodriguez, Barcenas, Waterman

playerNotes.note: In world-class form after a prolific Premier League season; the single most-cited player in the fixture and Ghana's whole attacking plan with Partey/Kudus out.
Captain, penalty taker and qualifying driving force (7G/7A in 10) at his third World Cup with 120 caps.
34-year-old surprise starter up front, 14 goals in 55 caps and Dimers' highest Panama scoring projection; now also Panama's on-pitch penalty option.
Captain in this XI (98 caps, 8 goals); a winger pushed forward, on free-kick and corner duty driving Panama's set pieces.
Key transition threat Christiansen trusts to create in the final third, plus free-kick and corner taker now Davis/Carrasquilla are benched.
Starts in central midfield with Godoy benched - a younger, more energetic legs option to screen and break up Ghana's link play.

win.reason: Markets price Colombia ~72% (-250/-267). Superior quality across the pitch vs Uzbekistan's World Cup debut, with the confirmed front of Suarez/Diaz/James/Arias the difference. Azteca altitude (~2240m) and a disciplined debutant low block temper the margin rather than the result.

scorers.note: Confirmed lone striker; Primeira Liga top scorer (38 in 53 for Sporting CP) on his tournament debut, the focal point of every Colombia attack.
Confirmed left wing; career-best form (15 G/14 A in a Bundesliga title season at Bayern); direct running targets the Uzbek right where Khusanov's match sharpness is questioned.
Confirmed No.10 and captain; creative hub, set-piece deliverer and primary penalty taker - value via free-kicks/penalty and late arrivals in the box.
Uzbekistan captain and all-time top scorer (44 in 92); joint Super Lig top scorer (22) - the only realistic Uzbek scoring threat, on the counter.

assists.note: Confirmed playmaker and set-piece deliverer; Colombia's chief chance-creator feeding Suarez and Diaz.
Confirmed left wing; 14 assists last season, his cutbacks and crosses a primary supply line to Suarez.
Confirmed overlapping right-back; high work-rate, repeated wide deliveries against a deep Uzbek block.

penalty.note: With Richard Rios out of the XI, James (captain, Colombia's established first-choice taker) is the clear penalty taker; Diaz the converting backup. Medium likelihood as Uzbekistan defend deep and concede fouls under sustained pressure.

lineups.home: Utkir Yusupov, Rustam Ashurmatov, Abdukodir Khusanov, Jakhongir Urozov, Farrukh Sayfiev, Otabek Shukurov, Akmal Mozgovoy, Sherzod Nasrullaev, Abbosbek Fayzullaev, Oston Urunov, Eldor Shomurodov
lineups.away: Camilo Vargas, Daniel Munoz, Davinson Sanchez, Jhon Lucumi, Johan Mojica, Jefferson Lerma, Gustavo Puerta, Jhon Arias, James Rodriguez, Luis Diaz, Luis Suarez

playerNotes.note: Confirmed striker; Sporting CP's 38-goal Primeira Liga golden boot, on his major-tournament debut he is the focal striker every move runs through.
Confirmed left wing in best form of his career off a Bundesliga title at Bayern (15 G/14 A); pace and dribbling test Uzbekistan's right side.
Confirmed No.10 and captain; the creative hub, set-piece and penalty taker - Colombia's tempo and final-third quality flow through him.
The change in the confirmed XI: partners Lerma in the double pivot ahead of the omitted Richard Rios; screens the back four and controls midfield at altitude.
Man City defender anchoring the (probable) Uzbek back three, but injury-curtailed minutes raise sharpness concerns against Diaz/Suarez.
Uzbekistan's talisman and record scorer; isolated as the lone counter outlet in a 3-4-2-1, dependent on Fayzullaev behind him.

win.reason: Czechia field both Schick and Hložek up top in a 3-5-2 with Krejčí an aerial set-piece weapon from the back three (he already headed home the opener vs South Korea), and the Czechs scored the most set-piece goals in UEFA qualifying (10). But South Africa under Broos defend in a disciplined deep block with elite keeper Williams behind it, and lost their openers only narrowly in shape, so a draw is genuinely live.

scorers.note: Leverkusen's form striker (16 Bundesliga goals, 9 in his last 7), Czech qualifying top-scorer and penalty taker; main threat but treated measured given SA's deep block and Williams behind it — not a banker.

Set-piece aerial weapon from the back three; already scored the headed opener vs South Korea and the Czechs convert heavily from corners/long throws — South Africa's biggest tracking problem at dead balls.
Leads the SA line in Foster's absence; 12 PSL goals for Sundowns this season at 0.76/90, the focal point for any counter-attacking break.
Partners Schick up top in the 3-5-2; mobile second striker who drops and runs the channels, a secondary finisher off Czech build-up and set-piece scraps.

assists.note: SA's most direct outlet — pace and width on the flank, draws fouls and delivers; also a free-kick/corner option, the likeliest creator on the break.
Right wing-back delivering crosses into a box stacked with Schick, Hložek and Krejčí; Czechia attempted the most crosses in qualifying (284).
Pace on the opposite flank; SA's counter-attack runs through Maseko and Appollis in transition feeding Rayners.

penalty.note: Schick is Czechia's designated penalty taker. For South Africa, with Mofokeng and Foster on the bench, Appollis is the on-pitch option (Mokoena has taken none in his career). Note Williams is an elite penalty-saver — first keeper to save four in an AFCON shootout, 2024 best-GK — which lowers the value of any Czech spot-kick.

lineups.home: 3-5-2: Kovář; Hranáč, Holeš, Krejčí; Coufal, Sadílek, Darida, Černý, Sojka; Hložek, Schick
lineups.away: 4-3-3: Williams; Mudau, Okon, Mbokazi, Modiba; Mokoena, Mbatha, Adams; Maseko, Rayners, Appollis

playerNotes.note: 30 and fully fit (no aging-decline case), top of his game with 16 Bundesliga goals; Czech penalty taker and main goal threat, but kept measured given SA's deep block.
Set-piece aerial threat from the back three; scored the opener vs South Korea and won the most defensive-third possessions of any UEFA qualifier — danger at both boxes.
Captain and elite shot-stopper; first keeper to save four penalties in an AFCON shootout (2024), ranked a top-10 world GK — anchors a disciplined deep block.
Deep-lying SA midfielder who strikes from distance and free kicks (scored a famous FK vs Morocco), but has never taken a penalty — set-piece delivery, not the pen man.
Starts up front with Foster benched; in-form Sundowns striker (12 league goals) tasked with leading the line and finishing rare chances on the break.
Dropped from the confirmed XI to the bench alongside Provod and Šulc; a major aerial set-piece game-changer Czechia can introduce, as SA can bring Mofokeng and Foster.

win.reason: Switzerland are unbeaten in competitive matches since late 2024 and conceded only twice in qualifying; Bosnia rely on a 40-year-old Dzeko and are nursing injury doubts (Tabakovic out, Kolasinac knock).

scorers.note: Leading Swiss scorer (24 intl goals) and penalty taker; converted from the spot vs Qatar in the opener.
Pacey wide threat cutting in; key chance-creator for Switzerland in the final third.
Direct left-sided winger who carries Switzerland's attacking output alongside Ndoye.
Bosnia's most mobile forward and Dzeko's foil; their likeliest source of a goal.

assists.note: Dictates Swiss tempo and set-piece delivery; the main creative hub.
Creates chances from left-back and takes set pieces for Switzerland.
Breakout PSV winger; Bosnia's chief creator and clutch performer in the play-offs.

penalty.note: Embolo took and scored Switzerland's penalty against Qatar; he is the established taker with Xhaka as senior backup.

lineups.home: Kobel; Zakaria, Akanji, Elvedi; Aebischer, Sow, Xhaka, Rodriguez; Ndoye, Embolo, Vargas
lineups.away: Vasilj; Kolasinac, Muharemovic, Katic, Dedic; Memic, Tahirovic, Basic, Bajraktarevic; Demirovic, Lukic

playerNotes.note: Leads the line and takes penalties; scored in the 1-1 opener vs Qatar.
Captain and metronome who controls tempo and dead balls.
Anchors a back three that conceded only twice across qualifying.
40-year-old talisman and record scorer (73 goals); rescued the play-off vs Wales but likely managed minutes.
Captain and left-back carrying a knock from the Canada draw; a fitness doubt to start.
Forward ruled out / not match fit due to an ankle injury, thinning Bosnia's attacking options.

win.reason: Confirmed Canada XI (Crepeau; Johnston, Cornelius, De Fougerolles, Laryea; Buchanan, Eustaquio, Kone, Ahmed; Larin, J.David) fields its full first-choice attack with Davies held in reserve, and at BC Place the co-hosts should dominate a Qatar 4-3-3 that mustered only 0.76 xG, 6 shots and 32% possession vs Switzerland. Opta sims Canada at 72.9%, books ~1.28-1.29. But Qatar's Madibo-Gaber-Laye screen plus the Khoukhi/Pedro Miguel block frustrated a Swiss side that ran up 3.24 xG and still only drew via a stoppage-time leveller, exactly the deep-block-out-xG'd-yet-survives pattern; Canada have never won a WC match in 7 tries and were wasteful vs Bosnia. Draw is live at +470, so DNB at ~1.55 is the honest price on a real ~55-60% clean Canada edge, not implied certainty.

scorers.note: Confirmed starter and Canada's penalty taker (scored twice from the spot vs Iceland in warm-up); shortest anytime price at +110. NOT a banker - poor recent form, 1 goal since early Feb at Juventus, and blazed a chance over vs Bosnia.
Now starts after his super-sub volley equaliser vs Bosnia; penalty backup behind David. Aging marquee striker - explicitly NOT a banker, streaky finisher reliant on service.
Confirmed RW, rediscovered form at Villarreal; cuts inside onto his left, secondary goal route if Qatar sit deep.

Qatar's confirmed talisman, pen + free-kick + corner taker; the only realistic away scorer on the counter. Qatar created almost nothing vs Switzerland (0.76 xG), so this is value-against-the-grain, not a likely event.

assists.note: Confirmed start over Davies/Millar - Vancouver Whitecaps academy product getting a homecoming start; ball-carrying creator from the left, a live assist source vs a deep block.
Whips crosses for David/Larin from the right; primary supply line against a low Qatar block.
Record 10 assists at 2019 Asian Cup; takes Qatar's corners/free-kicks, so any Qatar goal (incl. a Khoukhi/Pedro Miguel set-piece header) likely runs through him.

penalty.backup: Cyle Larin (Canada); Akram Afif for Qatar
penalty.note: Canada dominate the ball and David is the squad's most-fouled-in-box player + designated taker, so a Canada spot-kick is the most probable; if Qatar win one it's Afif. Deep-block congestion keeps this only moderate, not high.

lineups.home: Crepeau; Johnston, Cornelius, de Fougerolles, Laryea; Buchanan, Eustaquio, Kone, Ali Ahmed; J. David, Larin
lineups.away: Abunada; Al Oui, Pedro Miguel, Khoukhi, Homam El Amin; Madibo, Gaber, Issa Laye; Edmilson Junior, Abdurisag, Afif

playerNotes.note: FIT but named only on the bench - Marsch trusts the settled Buchanan/Ahmed wide pairing and is easing Davies back, so Canadas first-half ceiling is slightly lower than full strength; a strong 60-70min game-changer if Qatars block holds, which raises late-Canada-goal value.
Starts over Dayne St. Clair (Marsch called it the hardest decision of his career) - reliable but untested at this level, so any Crepeau error is a live tail risk on Qatars rare counters.
The pen taker and shortest anytime scorer (+110) but arrives in genuinely poor form (1 goal since early February) and missed a presentable chance vs Bosnia - do not treat him as a lock.
Earned this start off his super-sub equaliser vs Bosnia; an aging, service-dependent finisher - a sensible scorer flyer, never a banker.
Captain/CB who scored the stoppage-time leveller vs Switzerland - with Pedro Miguel he makes Qatar a genuine set-piece header threat off Afif deliveries, the main route to an away goal.

Top scorer in AFC qualifying, starts on the bench behind Abdurisag - a dangerous 60min+ introduction if Qatar chase, so a Qatar clean sheet AND a late Qatar goal both carry more weight than the lopsided xG suggests.

win.reason: Mexico are home in Guadalajara with a settled 4-3-3 and Edson Alvarez restoring midfield steel alongside Lira and Romo, plus Alvarado, Gutierrez and Quiñones giving width and creation around Jimenez. South Korea defend in a compact block off Kim Min-jae and counter through Son and Lee Kang-in, which makes this a genuine draw risk rather than a formality — Mexico are ~55% favourites, so the win is far from a certainty.

scorers.note: Leads the line and scored vs South Africa, but at 34 he is a finisher of chances, not a banker against a deep Korean block marshalled by Kim Min-jae.
Wide runner who gets in behind the Korean full-backs (Seol Young-woo, Kim Moon-hwan); a real cut-inside threat from the right channel.
Korea's primary goal threat and penalty taker, dangerous on the counter, but at 33 leading the line against Vasquez/Alvarez he is not a banker.
On the bench but Mexico's clearest impact sub — a genuine penalty-box striker who could be sent on to chase a goal against a tiring low block.

assists.note: Confirmed starter and a chief creator from the right/half-spaces feeding Jimenez and the runners.
Starting creator with line-breaking passing; key supply line into the box for Mexico.
Korea's chief creator and set-piece taker; his vision and delivery is the main route to feed Son on the break and from dead balls.

penalty.note: For Korea, Son is the established penalty taker with Lee Kang-in on free-kicks/backup. For Mexico, Raul Jimenez is the likely spot-kick taker, with Alvarado as an alternative; medium likelihood given Mexico's pressure into a packed box.

lineups.home: Rangel; Sanchez, Vasquez, Alvarez, Gallardo; Lira, Romo, Gutierrez; Alvarado, Quiñones, Jimenez
lineups.away: Kim Seung-gyu; Seol Young-woo, Kim Min-jae, Lee Han-beom, Kim Moon-hwan; Lee Gi-hyuk, Hwang In-beom, Paik Seung-ho; Lee Kang-in, Lee Jae-sung, Son Heung-min

playerNotes.note: Steps in for suspended Cesar Montes (red vs South Africa); his ball-winning and aerial presence shore up the back line and let Lira/Romo push.
Comes in for Alvaro Fidalgo to add legs and progression in the engine room of the 4-3-3.
Lone striker who scored in the 2-0 opener; focal point but reliant on service against a deep block.
Captain and talisman leading the line with Hwang Hee-chan benched; Korea's main outlet on the counter and penalty/free-kick threat.
Scored and assisted in the 2-1 comeback over Czechia; the deep-lying metronome who springs Korean counters to Son and Lee Kang-in.
Bayern anchor of the Korean back line; his organisation and aerial dominance are the wall Mexico must break to avoid a draw.

win.reason: USA dominate possession at home in Seattle and beat Paraguay 4-1, but Pulisic OUT (calf) removes their top creator/scorer, and Australia's youngest-ever XI sits in a compact back-five low block built to counter. ~52-55% USA, with a real draw/upset tail (~45%); not a certainty.

scorers.note: Led the line and scored a brace vs Paraguay; now USA's focal striker and a co-penalty option with Pulisic out. Faces a packed Souttar/Burgess box, so far from a lock.
Promoted to start in Pulisic's place; sharp box-poacher and interchangeable with Balogun on pens. Good value as a fresh starter against a deep block.
Operates as the advanced No.10/inside-forward inheriting much of Pulisic's creative+shooting load; arrives late into the box.
Australia's counter outlet and Türkiye scorer; pace in transition makes him the away side's most likely scorer if USA over-commit.

assists.note: Overlapping left-back is USA's main width and cut-back source against a low block now that Pulisic isn't hugging that flank.
Box-crashing central midfielder; deliveries and second-phase set-piece knockdowns feed Balogun/Pepi.
Primary line-breaking passer between the lines feeding the front line; through-balls to Balogun were a feature vs Paraguay.

penalty.note: Pulisic (13/15, USA's designated taker) is OUT, so duty shifts to Balogun, with Pepi interchangeable per RotoWire/Yahoo. Australia defending deep with a back-five raises box-contact/handball risk, keeping pen odds live but not high.

lineups.home: Freese; Dest, Richards, Ream (C), Robinson; Adams, McKennie, Freeman; Tillman, Pepi, Balogun (4-3-3)
lineups.away: Beach; Italiano, Circati, Souttar (C), Burgess, Bos; O'Neill, Metcalfe, Okon-Engstler; Irankunda, Toure (5-4-1 / 3-4-3 hybrid)

playerNotes.note: RULED OUT with a calf injury kicked in the Paraguay win; he is USA's primary scorer and designated penalty taker (13/15) — his absence reshapes the scorer, assist and penalty markets.
Direct replacement in the starting XI; box-finisher and co-penalty option, adds a second genuine striker threat alongside Balogun.
In red-hot form after a brace vs Paraguay; now the focal striker and likely first-choice penalty taker, but no banker against a 5-man block.
22-year-old keeper kept a clean sheet with 8 saves vs Türkiye on debut; in form and the key to any Australia clean-sheet/under-2.5 outcome.
Captain and aerial anchor of the deep back-five; a set-piece threat at the other end and the reason USA's correct-score/win-to-nil lines are downgraded.
Scored vs Türkiye; Australia's main counter-attacking outlet whose pace can out-xG a dominant USA if they leave space in transition.

win.reason: Opta makes Morocco ~55% favourites on superior attacking quality (Brahim Diaz, Saibari, El Khannouss, Hakimi's overlaps), but that is no lock. Scotland, top of Group C after a gritty 1-0 over Haiti, are set up in a packed deep block with five defensive bodies and Adams alone up top - a draw suits them for qualification. Morocco themselves only drew Brazil 1-1 despite dominating territory. Draw + Scotland upside is roughly 45%, so a straight Morocco win is closer to a coin-flip-plus than a certainty - draw / under-2.5 carry genuine weight.

scorers.note: AFCON Golden Boot (5 goals), leads Morocco's attack and is primary penalty/set-piece taker - but he missed the AFCON final penalty and faces a packed Scotland block, so not a banker.
Scored Morocco's opener vs Brazil; arrives late from the front line into the box, a real threat against a deep block.
Scotland's lone striker and main outlet on the counter; chances are scarce against Morocco's back four but he is the Scot most likely to convert a transition.
Scotland's set-piece target and crucial-goal specialist; Robertson/McGinn deliveries aim for his head - their likeliest scorer from a dead ball.

assists.note: Captain and overlapping right-back who pushes high; primary cross/cutback supplier and joint set-piece taker for Morocco.
Creative midfielder working the half-spaces with defence-splitting passes - Morocco's chance-creation hub against a low block.
Scotland captain and main corner/wide free-kick taker, feeding McTominay - their likeliest assist source from set pieces.

penalty.note: Diaz is Morocco's confirmed primary penalty taker but missed the AFCON final spot-kick, so not nailed-on; Hakimi is the experienced backup. Scotland would use McTominay/Christie. Morocco's territorial dominance vs a packed box makes a spot-kick plausible.

lineups.home: Gunn; Patterson, Hendry, Hanley, Tierney, Robertson; Christie, Ferguson, McTominay, McGinn; Adams (5-4-1 deep block) - Doak, Hickey, Shankland benched; McKenna out (calf)
lineups.away: Bono; Hakimi, Mazraoui, Diop, Chadi Riad; Bouaddi, Ounahi, El Khannouss; Saibari, Brahim Diaz, El Aynaoui (4-3-3) - unchanged from the Brazil draw; Mazraoui's shoulder doubt resolved

playerNotes.note: Talisman leading the line; AFCON top scorer and primary penalty + set-piece taker. Carries Morocco's goal threat but faces a deep block and missed the AFCON final pen - dangerous, not guaranteed.
On the scoresheet vs Brazil; late-arriving runner who can break a low block.
Captain, attacking right-back; main width/overlap and joint dead-ball taker - Morocco's chief supply line against Scotland's compact shape.
Scotland's engine and aerial set-piece target; their single biggest goal threat comes from runs onto Robertson/McGinn deliveries.
Lone striker tasked with holding up and leading sparse counters; Scotland's main transition outlet now that Doak is benched.
Captain at left-back, anchors the block and takes the bulk of corners/wide free kicks - key both to keeping it tight and to Scotland's only real attacking deliveries.

win.reason: Brazil field a near-full-strength 4-3-3 (Alisson; Danilo, Marquinhos, Gabriel Magalhaes, Douglas Santos; Casemiro, Bruno Guimaraes, Paqueta; Raphinha, Vinicius Jr, Cunha) vs a Haiti side set up in a deep 5-2-3/4-2-4 hybrid built to absorb and counter through Pierrot. Brazil should win, but their 1-1 opener vs Morocco was flat (Vinicius the only spark, Raphinha isolated), and a disciplined low block plus Placide in goal makes a frustrating draw or 1-0 grind a genuine, non-trivial outcome - this is not a formality.

scorers.note: Scored Brazil's only goal vs Morocco; isolating Haiti's confirmed right side (Arcus/Duverne) is Brazil's clearest route, but a packed box dampens any 'banker' claim.
Confirmed starter through the middle in place of Igor Thiago; mobile false-9 movement vs a deep Ade/Delcroix line, but service can dry up against a low block.
Brazil's primary penalty taker with Neymar out and a set-piece/corner option; was quiet and isolated vs Morocco, so form is a caveat despite the goal threat.
Haiti's confirmed lone-ish centre-forward and main counter outlet; an against-the-run-of-play or set-piece header is Haiti's realistic path to the scoresheet.

assists.note: Set-piece and corner duty plus open-play creation from the right; most likely Brazil creator vs a deep block.
Cut-backs from the left after beating Arcus/Duverne are Brazil's prime chance-creation source, but a crowded box limits clean assists.
Advanced of the Casemiro-Bruno pivot; through-balls and set-piece deliveries make him a credible creator if Brazil break the block.

penalty.note: With Neymar out (calf), Raphinha is Brazil's confirmed primary penalty taker, Vinicius the backup. Haiti's deep, physical block invites box contact, so a spot-kick is a real possibility, though not high given Haiti's disciplined low defending.

lineups.home: Brazil (4-3-3): Alisson; Danilo, Marquinhos, Gabriel Magalhaes, Douglas Santos; Casemiro, Bruno Guimaraes, Lucas Paqueta; Raphinha, Matheus Cunha, Vinicius Junior. (Neymar OUT - calf; Cunha in for Igor Thiago; Danilo for Ibanez; Douglas Santos at LB.)
lineups.away: Haiti (5-2-3 / 4-2-4 deep block): Johnny Placide; Carlens Arcus, Ricardo Ade, Hannes Delcroix, Martin Experience, Jean-Kevin Duverne; Jean-Ricner Bellegarde, Danley Jean Jacques; Ruben Providence, Frantzdy Pierrot, Josue Casimir. (Duckens Nazon and Wilson Isidor on the bench.)

playerNotes.note: Confirmed starting left wing; scored Brazil's only goal in the 1-1 vs Morocco and is the focal point attacking Haiti's confirmed right side of Arcus and Duverne.
Confirmed at left-back (not Wesley/Vanderson as pre-match speculation suggested); his overlaps feed Vinicius and stretch Haiti's deep block.
Confirmed centre-forward in place of Igor Thiago; his dropping movement aims to unlock the Ade-Delcroix central pairing.
Confirmed right wing and Brazil's primary penalty/set-piece taker with Neymar out; was too quiet and isolated vs Morocco, so a response is needed.
Confirmed leading the line; Haiti's main counter-attacking and aerial outlet - the player most likely to threaten Brazil against the run of play.
Confirmed in goal behind a five-man defensive block; his shot-stopping is central to Haiti's plan to frustrate Brazil and keep the score low.

win.reason: Confirmed XIs sharpen the picture: Yildiz starts and Muldur & Akgun come in for Turkiye, who out-create everyone (30 shots, 72% vs Australia) but lost their opener 0-2. Paraguay set up as a deep-block counter side - Cubas screening Alderete & G.Gomez, Almiron/Enciso breaking, Pitta alone up top after the 1-4 USA loss. Turkiye are favourites on possession and quality, but a possession-favourite vs a compact counter side is a REAL draw risk: ~48-52% only, and a low-scoring stalemate is very live.

scorers.note: Confirmed to START after a calf scare kept him out the opener; Turkiye's most direct ball-carrier from the left, takes shots on early - prime tool to unlock a low block.
Leads the line / cuts in off the flank; led Turkiye's shot volume vs Australia and a constant left-footed threat against a deep defence.
Captain, penalty + free-kick taker; arrives in the box and strikes from range - if Paraguay concede a dead ball or spot-kick he is the man.
Paraguay's confirmed lone striker (in for Sanabria); a physical hold-up focal point and their best chance to convert a rare counter or set-piece.

assists.note: Chief creator and set-piece deliverer; corners and the final ball are Turkiye's main supply line to break a packed Paraguay box.
Overlapping left-back pushing high for cutbacks as Turkiye stretch the deep block; width matters against a compact four.
Paraguay's transition outlet; pace and directness make him the likeliest to spring a counter and feed Pitta or Enciso.

penalty.note: Turkiye's sustained pressure on Paraguay's deep, foul-prone block keeps a spot-kick live; Calhanoglu is the ice-cold designated taker, Akturkoglu the backup. Live but not strong.

lineups.home: Cakir; Muldur, Demiral, Bardakci, Kadioglu; Calhanoglu, Yuksek; Akgun, Guler, Yildiz; Akturkoglu (4-2-3-1)
lineups.away: Gill; Caceres, G.Gomez, Alderete, Alonso; Cubas, D.Gomez; Almiron, Galarza, Enciso; Pitta (4-2-3-1)

playerNotes.note: CONFIRMED START - returns after a calf problem; his ball-carrying from the left is Turkiye's best tool to unlock a packed defence.
Comes in at right-back for Zeki Celik; provides overlapping width down the right alongside Akgun.
Drafted in (Kokcu/Baris Alper Yilmaz drop to the bench); direct wide runner to stretch Paraguay's block.
Captain and metronome; primary penalty, corner and free-kick taker - Turkiye go as he goes.
Captain and aerial menace from set-pieces; anchors the deep block and is Paraguay's likeliest scorer from a dead ball.
Confirmed lone striker ahead of Sanabria; physical focal point with Almiron, Galarza & Enciso feeding off the counter.

win.reason: Netherlands edge possession (~55% win prob) and have the deeper squad, but they were held 2-2 by Japan and look exposed at the back without Jurrien Timber (groin, out). Sweden's back-three with the elite Isak–Gyökeres front two is a genuine counter threat, so DNB hedges the live draw/upset risk rather than backing a straight Dutch win at short odds.

scorers.note: Netherlands' on-the-pitch penalty taker with Depay benched; cuts in from the left and is the most reliable Dutch goal route, but no anytime-scorer is a banker in a tight counter game.
Sweden's primary penalty taker and focal point; thrives on transition against a high Dutch line missing Timber — but service may be limited if Sweden sit deep, so not a banker.
Pace to exploit van Hecke/van de Ven on the counter; the Japan game showed the Dutch back line is vulnerable in behind. Genuine threat, not nailed-on.
In-form Roma winger starting on the right; gets into the box but shares the load with Gakpo and Summerville — speculative.

assists.note: Dutch deep-lying creator who progresses play through the lines; most likely to thread the final ball if Netherlands break Sweden down.
Overlapping right wing-back delivering crosses for Gakpo/Malen; Netherlands' main wide supply down that flank.
Left wing-back on Sweden corner/free-kick duty with Elanga benched; set-piece and counter delivery to Gyökeres/Isak.

penalty.note: With Memphis Depay on the bench, Gakpo is Netherlands' penalty taker from the start (scored two vs Uzbekistan, multiple recent pens); Reijnders/free-kick backup. Gyökeres is Sweden's confirmed primary, having won and converted in qualifying. Two high-press/counter teams means a spot-kick is plausible but not likely.

lineups.home: Bart Verbruggen, Denzel Dumfries, Virgil van Dijk, Micky van de Ven, Jan Paul van Hecke, Frenkie de Jong, Tijjani Reijnders, Ryan Gravenberch, Donyell Malen, Cody Gakpo, Crysencio Summerville
lineups.away: Kristoffer Nordfeldt, Gustaf Lagerbielke, Victor Lindelöf, Isak Hien, Yasin Ayari, Jesper Karlström, Anton Bernhardsson, Gabriel Gudmundsson, Benjamin Nygren, Viktor Gyökeres, Alexander Isak

playerNotes.note: Started the Japan opener and was involved in a ricochet for Nakamura's goal; with Timber out (groin) the Dutch back four looks shaky and van Hecke is the weak link Isak/Gyökeres will target in behind.
Now Netherlands' penalty taker with Depay benched for fitness; also on corners. Cutting in from the left he is the Dutch side's most consistent goal threat and a key creative outlet.
Sits in the Dutch three-man midfield as the chief progressor; must control tempo to break a deep Swedish block, and is the most likely assist source.
Sweden's confirmed primary penalty taker and counter spearhead; fired Sweden to the finals (3-2 vs Poland) and converted in qualifying. Lethal on transition against a high Dutch line.
Pairs with Gyökeres in Sweden's 3-5-2; pace and finishing to punish Dutch lapses in behind — exactly the vulnerability Japan exposed. Sweden's draw/upset hopes ride on this front two.

Left wing-back back in the XI after cramp in the opener; with Elanga dropped to the bench he takes on more of Sweden's corner/free-kick delivery alongside Karlström and provides counter-attacking width.

win.reason: Germany confirmed at full strength in a 4-2-3-1 (Sane-Musiala-Wirtz behind Havertz) after a 7-1 rout of Curacao, but Ivory Coast's compact block conceded just one shot on target vs Ecuador and carry real counter venom through Diomande and Amad Diallo. Roughly 55-58% Germany, so fair odds sit longer than the market's ~1.50; the draw and Ivory-Coast-not-to-lose lines deserve genuine weight.

scorers.note: Lone striker; brace (incl. a penalty) vs Curacao and on penalty duty, but a deep Ivorian block limits central chances - strong option, not a banker.
Scored vs Curacao; dribbling built to unpick a packed defence, though space will be tight against a low block.
Free No.10 who set up Germany's opener; arrives late into the box and shoots from the edge - the chance a low block concedes.
Scored the 90th-minute winner vs Ecuador and now starts; Ivory Coast's main transition outlet into the space behind Brown/Kimmich.

assists.note: Captain and primary set-piece/corner taker; assisted Musiala vs Curacao, the main creative supply against a deep block.
Germany's chief chance creator; combined for the opener vs Curacao and links constantly with Havertz and Sane.
Ivory Coast's standout vs Ecuador; pace and trickery down the flank to feed Bonny or Amad on the counter.

penalty.note: Havertz converted Germany's penalty vs Curacao and is first choice; relentless pressure on a packed box raises spot-kick odds, but Ivory Coast defend deep and disciplined, so it is not high-probability.

lineups.home: Manuel Neuer, Jonathan Tah, Joshua Kimmich, Nico Schlotterbeck, Nathaniel Brown, Aleksandar Pavlovic, Felix Nmecha, Leroy Sane, Jamal Musiala, Florian Wirtz, Kai Havertz
lineups.away: Yahia Fofana, Odilon Kossounou, Wilfried Singo, Emmanuel Agbadou, Ghislain Konan, Franck Kessie, Ibrahim Sangare, Christ Inao Oulai, Yan Diomande, Ange-Yoan Bonny, Amad Diallo

playerNotes.note: Starts as lone striker after a brace (one penalty) vs Curacao and holds penalty duty, but faces a packed central area from Agbadou and Kossounou.
Captains from right-back as primary set-piece taker; assisted Musiala vs Curacao and is Germany's creative and tempo hub against the low block.
Makes his competitive bow leading the line - the Inter Milan forward is a mobile transition focal point, with Sebastien Haller out of the squad entirely.
Promoted to the XI after his 90th-minute winner vs Ecuador; pace and dribbling are Ivory Coast's principal counter threat behind Brown and Kimmich.
Kept a clean sheet behind a disciplined block vs Ecuador (only one shot on target conceded), underlining the opponent-clean-sheet and under-2.5 risk.
Roams freely with Musiala off Havertz as Germany's creative pair; both scored/created vs Curacao and are most likely to unlock Ivory Coast's compact 4-3-3.

win.reason: Confirmed teamsheets sharpen the read: Curacao have set up in a maximal 5-4-1 (Room behind a back five of Brenet, Obispo, Gaari, Floranus, Fonville) with Locadia isolated up top — an explicit park-the-bus to limit a 7-goal-leaking defence after the Germany battering. Ecuador (3-1-4-2: Caicedo shielding a Franco-Hincapie-Pacho back three; Alcivar and Vite the central runners; Plata and Enner Valencia paired up top) are heavy favourites (DraftKings ECU -600 ML, -2.5 spread) and dominated Ivory Coast (lost 1-0 late) without a clinical edge. Against a deliberate ten-behind-the-ball block, Ecuador's bluntness is the live risk: the draw/draw-no-bet/under-2.5 and Curacao-clean-sheet markets retain genuine value, and the win-to-nil cricket-score lines are downgraded — a possession-favourite vs a deep-counter underdog is a real draw risk.

scorers.note: Confirmed to start as one of the front two; Flamengo forward and Ecuador's most consistent creator-scorer. Cutting in against an overloaded five-man block, he is the most reliable goal source — though the 5-4-1 wall caps even his ceiling.
Confirmed starter, all-time WC top scorer and first-choice penalty taker — but at 36 a veteran finisher, explicitly NOT a banker; deep blocks starve a target CF of clean service.
Confirmed to start wide in the 3-1-4-2; led Ecuador for shots vs Ivory Coast (3). Direct runner who can shoot from range if the block stays compact.
Confirmed in the central midfield four; a late-arriving runner into the box is a classic way to unlock a packed defence, and he was among Ecuador's most accurate passers vs Ivory Coast.

assists.note: Primary chance-creator from wide and on set-piece deliveries; most likely to register an assist whether or not he scores against a deep-sitting Curacao.
CONFIRMED in the XI as the left-sided wing-back (no longer a doubt) — his overlapping crosses are Ecuador's main width source against a back five.
Confirmed at the base of midfield; line-breaking passes from deep are the key supply route to thread a low block.

penalty.note: Curacao defend deep in a 5-4-1 under sustained pressure, raising spot-kick odds. Valencia is the confirmed taker (scored the qualifying winner vs Argentina); with Kendry Paez on the bench, Caicedo is the in-XI backup.

lineups.home: Galindez, Franco, Hincapie, Pacho, Caicedo, Alcivar, Vite, Yeboah, Estupinan, Plata, Enner Valencia
lineups.away: Room, Brenet, Obispo, Gaari, Floranus, Fonville, L. Bacuna, J. Bacuna, Comenencia, Chong, Locadia

playerNotes.note: Confirmed to start up top; Ecuador's all-time leading WC scorer (6) and designated penalty taker in his third and final World Cup.
Confirmed at the base of the 3-1-4-2; Chelsea engine who controls tempo and must break a 5-4-1 with line-breaking passes.
Confirmed in the front two; Ecuador's most dangerous creator/scorer and the key name on both goal and assist markets.
Confirmed lone striker in the 5-4-1; will be isolated and feed on counters — Curacao's only real outball.
Confirmed to start; scored Curacao's first-ever World Cup goal vs Germany and led them for shots — their most likely source of a counter or set-piece goal.
Confirmed captain alongside brother Juninho in the midfield four; organises the low block, recycles possession and takes set pieces.

win.reason: Japan are clearly the stronger side technically and come off a creditable 2-2 draw with the Netherlands, while Tunisia were dismantled 5-1 by Sweden and are reeling under interim direction. However Tunisia are organised when they sit deep and a must-win game can produce a low-block, counter-attacking setup that frustrates Japan; their defensive collapse v Sweden was partly self-inflicted rather than structural. Roughly a 55% Japan win, with real draw and low-scoring equity, so this is favouritism not certainty.

scorers.note: Central striker and one of Japan's designated penalty takers; main aerial and box presence against a defence that conceded five.
Japan's chief creator on free kicks and corners; cuts inside from the right to shoot if fit after the knee scare.
Secondary penalty taker and inside-forward threat; reliable finisher arriving from wide areas.
Attacking midfielder with late runs into the box and free-kick duty; arrives second phase.

assists.note: Primary set-piece deliverer (corners and free kicks) plus open-play chance creation; most assists likely to flow through him.
Wide creator and corner option; supplies cutbacks for the central striker.
Attacking full-back/wing-back overlapping into crossing positions on the right.

penalty.note: Japan's territorial dominance against a deep Tunisian block raises spot-kick odds; Ueda and Doan are the listed takers, with Doan often stepping up.

lineups.home: Aymen Dahmen, Yan Valery, Montassar Talbi, Dylan Bronn, Mohamed Ben Ouanes, Ellyes Skhiri, Anis Ben Slimane, Hannibal Mejbri, Elias Achouri, Elias Saad, Mohamed Ali Ben Romdhane
lineups.away: Zion Suzuki, Yukinari Sugawara, Ko Itakura, Hiroki Ito, Yuto Nagatomo, Kaishu Sano, Ao Tanaka, Takefusa Kubo, Daichi Kamada, Ritsu Doan, Ayase Ueda

playerNotes.note: Captain dropped from the matchday squad with a left-foot injury; Ko Itakura now wears the armband.
Knee knock in the 2-2 Netherlands draw left him a fitness doubt; if unavailable Sugawara starts and Doan pushes forward.
Headline absentee through injury, removing a key wide threat from the attack.
Lone striker and penalty taker; principal goal threat against a leaky Tunisia back line.
Scored Tunisia's only goal in the 5-1 loss to Sweden; rotation/role under review after defensive struggles.
Tunisia's key ball-carrier and outlet on the counter; their main hope of springing transitions if they sit deep.
